properties.title: Cyprus election sees parliament fragment as anti-establishment parties gain ground
properties.summary: NICOSIA — The Cypriot parliament will become more fragmented following Sunday’s national elections, with the far right gaining ground and anti-establishment newcomers entering the scene. With all votes counted , the center-right Democratic Rally party (DISY) came first with some 27.2 percent support and managed to hold its 17 seats in parliament, followed by the communist Progressive Party of Working People with 23.9 percent and 15 seats. The far-right National Popular Front (ELAM) party, an off

properties.title: The Guardian view on Erdoğan’s tightening grip on Turkey: the next election is already being decided | Editorial
properties.summary: The removal of an opposition party leader and closure of a liberal university show an authoritarian democracy moving closer to one-man rule Turkey’s next presidential election is scheduled for 2028. Many think it will come sooner. But by the time ballots are actually cast, the outcome may already have been decided – especially after the last few days. On Thursday, an appeals court removed the head of the opposition Republican People’s party (CHP), Özgür Özel, by annulling its 2023 leadershi

properties.title: Turkish police use tear gas to break into CHP headquarters
properties.summary: Turkish riot police used tear gas and forced their way into the Ankara headquarters of the main opposition CHP party after a court dismissed its leadership, as supporters blocked entrances in defiance of the ruling.

properties.title: Turkish police storm main opposition headquarters with tear gas
properties.summary: Officers used tear gas inside the CHP building in Ankara to enforce a controversial judicial decision reinstating former leader Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu. The intrusion has sparked fierce resistance from current party officials, who accuse Kılıçdaroğlu of deploying "mafia thugs" to seize power.

properties.title: Europe races to make it harder for Trump to rattle NATO
properties.summary: PRAGUE — Europe is trying to Trump-proof itself. Donald Trump’s whiplash over U.S. troop deployments in Poland turned this week’s GLOBSEC Forum in Prague into a preview of how Europe is working to keep NATO functioning even when Washington becomes a source of uncertainty . “Think of how confused the Russians are,” Polish Foreign Minister Radosław Sikorski joked at the POLITICO Speakeasy , after being asked how Warsaw could plan around Washington’s shifts. Sikorski called the troop episode “some

properties.title: EU’s public debt could become ‘explosive’ without action, IMF warns
properties.summary: NICOSIA — European Union’s public debt trajectory could become “explosive,” hurting the bloc’s economy, if no action is taken to deal with fiscal pressures, the International Monetary Fund told EU finance ministers on Saturday. “If left unchecked, public debt will be on an ​unsustainable path. Under unchanged policy, debt of the average European country would reach 130 percent ​of GDP by 2040 — roughly doubling from today,” the IMF said in a paper ⁠presented to the finance mini

properties.title: Poland’s fugitive former justice minister can’t rest easy in the US
properties.summary: PRAGUE — Poland is examining whether former Justice Minister Zbigniew Ziobro broke the rules by fleeing to the U.S. despite facing charges back home over accusations of misusing public funds, Polish Foreign Minister Radosław Sikorski said at Saturday’s POLITICO Speakeasy at the GLOBSEC Forum. Ziobro, who is still an MP and deputy chairman of Poland’s opposition Law and Justice party, “needs to face the charges,” Sikorski said. Ziobro had been in Hungary since 2025 after former Prime Minist

properties.title: Lithuania calls for frozen Russian assets to return to Ukraine funding debate
properties.summary: PRAGUE — Lithuanian Foreign Minister Kęstutis Budrys said Europe should put frozen Russian assets back on the table as it looks for ways to sustain Ukraine financially and increase pressure on Moscow to negotiate a peace deal. Speaking at the POLITICO Speakeasy at Prague’s GLOBSEC on Saturday, Budrys said last year’s agreement on a €90 billion loan for Ukraine was a useful step, but not a final answer to Kyiv’s long-term needs. “It’s not the end,” Budrys said of the frozen assets debate, arguing

properties.title: Magyar to visit Brussels next week to discuss unfreezing funds for Hungary
properties.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar will travel to Brussels next week to discuss unlocking billions in frozen EU funds with the European Commission, the country’s foreign minister said Saturday. “One of our most important tasks in the first couple of months is that we will get access to the recovery and resilience funds, which is a €10.4 billion envelope for Hungary,” Anita Orbán, who was sworn in as Hungary’s foreign minister earlier this month, told POLITICO in an interview at the GLOBSEC co

properties.title: Russia’s drone blame game fails to split Ukraine from its Baltic allies
properties.summary: PRAGUE — Russia is trying to turn a string of stray combat drones appearing over the Baltic countries into a political crisis between Ukraine and some of its staunchest allies. So far, the effort appears to have fallen flat. After a wave of drone incidents across Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania — blamed on Russia using electronic warfare to redirect Ukrainian drones into NATO airspace — Moscow accused the three Baltic countries of allowing Ukraine to use their airspace for attacks on Russia and th

properties.title: ‘Ups and downs’: Czech president admits rocky relationship with PM
properties.summary: Czech President Petr Pavel admitted he had a rocky relationship with Prime Minister Andrej Babiš — but said he was willing to try and find compromises. Asked how he would describe his ties with the populist leader, he told the POLITICO Speakeasy at the GLOBSEC forum Friday: “I would say realistic, which means there are ups and downs. “We have a number of meetings and I believe we will continue to have practical communications,” the retired general said, adding jokingly, “

properties.title: Trump fan Janša wins new term as Slovenia’s prime minister
properties.summary: Slovenia’s National Assembly on Friday handed nationalist politician Janez Janša his fourth term as prime minister. Earlier this week, Janša announced that his Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) had formed a coalition with several right-wing parties in the Assembly, clearing a path to Slovenia’s top job. Janša was backed by 51 out of 87 deputies who voted in the National Assembly on Friday. The return of Janša, an longtime admirer of U.S. President Donald Trump, gives right-wing populi

properties.title: US concerned Russian campaign against Baltics could ‘spark into something bigger’, says Rubio – as it happened
properties.summary: The US secretary of state said the US is monitoring Russian allegations against the Baltics Rubio begins with thanks to Sweden for hosting the ministerial meeting. But that’s where the niceties end as says the upcoming Ankara summit will be “one of the more important leaders’ summit in the history of Nato,” as the leaders will have to respond to Trump’s “disappointment” with the alliance’s “response to our operations in the Middle East.” “That will have to be addressed, that won’t be solved or a

properties.title: Marco Rubio: Trump ‘disappointment’ with Nato will be discussed at summit
properties.summary: Secretary of state suggests July meeting of alliance will be fraught as US demands help in strait of Hormuz Donald Trump is disappointed that Nato allies refused to become more actively involved in attacking Iran, the US secretary of state has said, setting up what could become a fraught summit of the alliance in July. Marco Rubio, meeting with foreign ministers of the military alliance, emphasised that he expected the rift would be discussed at the July meeting in Ankara, making the summit “one

properties.title: Top EU prosecutor complains to Brussels about Greece amid farm fraud probe
properties.summary: European Prosecutor Laura Codruța Kövesi sent a formal complaint to the European Commission regarding Greek moves seeking to sideline EU prosecutors from investigating lawmakers. Earlier this week, Greece’s government submitted an amendment to effectively block European prosecutors from investigating parliamentarians — a highly sensitive subject because of a major probe into political involvement in a massive farm fraud scandal . Members of Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis’ conservative

properties.title: The Dreamed Adventure review – beautiful but opaque Bulgarian tale of digging up the past
properties.summary: Cannes film festival: Valeska Grisebach’s complex drama tracks an archaeologist whose mountain dig is interrupted by an old friend with rather dirtier hands The digging up of the past – and the hiding of secrets in the present – are the themes of Valeska Grisebach’s complex, subtle, opaque new drama which seems to withhold some of its narrative meaning from the audience, moment-by-moment. It is set, like her previous film Western , in Bulgaria’s remote and beautiful mountainous country, where me

properties.title: Rutte says Zelenskyy will attend NATO summit in Ankara
properties.summary: HELSINGBORG, Sweden — NATO has formally invited Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to its upcoming leaders’ summit in Ankara, alliance chief Mark Rutte said Friday. “I invited him already, yes I did — he will be there,” Rutte said in response to a question from POLITICO in the southern city of Helsingborg during a meeting of NATO foreign ministers. The invite comes despite the U.S. previously resisting efforts to extend invitations to non-NATO partners , including Ukraine, as well as Austra

properties.title: Le Pen says NATO should pivot toward fighting Islamism
properties.summary: PARIS — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen said Friday that NATO should focus on combating radical Islamism as the alliance grapples with Russia’s war in Ukraine and growing concerns over U.S. disengagement from Europe. “NATO [should] reorient itself toward the fight against Islamist fundamentalism, which I believe is one of greatest dangers facing all the countries,” she said in an interview with French channel BFMTV. NATO was founded in 1949 to provide a collective defense

properties.title: Former French PM Gabriel Attal officially launches presidential bid
properties.summary: PARIS — Former French Prime Minister Gabriel Attal announced Friday that he will join the increasingly crowded race for president next year. “Since I profoundly love France and I profoundly love the French people, yes, I have decided to be a candidate to the presidency of the Republic,” Attal, the leader of French President Emmanuel Macron’s party, Renaissance, said at the conclusion of an event in the picturesque village of Mur-de-Barrez in southern France. The 37-year-old politician must now m

properties.title: Greece protesters marched in Athens outside Israeli embassy
properties.summary: Around 1,000 pro-Palestinian demonstrators marched through Athens on Thursday after Israël intercepted a flotilla carrying Gaza activists, gathering outside the Israeli embassy as international criticism over the arrests mounted.

properties.title: Rutte and Polish officials cheer Trump’s U-turn on NATO troops in Europe
properties.summary: HELSINGBORG, Sweden — NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte on Friday praised U.S. President Donald Trump’s abrupt announcement to deploy 5,000 U.S. troops to Poland — a dramatic change on last week’s move to cut the presence of American forces in the country. He was echoed by Polish politicians celebrating the policy shift. Speaking on Friday to reporters in Sweden during a NATO foreign ministers’ meeting, Rutte said he “welcomed” the announcement and confirmed military commanders were

properties.title: Erdoğan signs decree closing Istanbul's Bilgi University amid criminal probe into owner
properties.summary: As part of an investigation into Can Holding, the operating licence of Istanbul Bilgi University was revoked after its founding foundation was placed under trusteeship. The decision has raised concerns about the future of its students and academic staff.

properties.title: Turkey welcomes 422 Gaza flotilla activists after Israel detention
properties.summary: 422 Gaza flotilla activists landed in Istanbul on Thursday after being deported by Israel. Several alleged physical abuse and torture following the interception of their aid convoy.

properties.title: News live: Australian flotilla activists released by Israel arrive in Istanbul and claim they were denied food and water
properties.summary: Eleven Australians were among pro-Palestine activists detained by Israel from the Global Sumud flotilla. properties.title: US sending 5,000 troops to Poland as it draws down forces in Germany
properties.summary: President Donald Trump said Thursday that the U.S. would deploy an additional 5,000 troops to Poland, an apparent reversal of his moves to reduce the presence of American forces in Europe  to punish NATO  for a lack of support with the Iran war. Trump made the troop announcement in a social media post with few details, suggesting it was connected to the election last year of nationalist President Karol Nawrocki. The announcement came shortly after his administration  abruptly canc
